goal: Lykia (MegaByter cart) crashes to PC=$0002 in TRX64 but runs in the C64RE TS oracle. Find + fix the TRX64 CPU-core (or memory-read) divergence via a TS<->TRX64 first-divergence trace.
start_when: CLI-feel loop DONE and Alex gives the go
oracle: C64RE TypeScript runtime (runs Lykia fully); TRX64 Rust is the one that crashes

repro.good_result: boots to Lykia / Play / Prologue menu (TS)

ruled_out: MegaByter cart layer (cart.rs:1264) — 1:1 port of cartridge.ts:1060, read-diffed
bus $8000 RAM-fallthrough (full.rs:707), PLA reconfig on $DE02, build_linear_chip_data
MegaByter banking (probe-713-devcore green; register_02/current_bank stay 0 at crash)
scope_files: crates/trx64-core/src/full.rs (only if divergence is a READ)

tasks.title: deterministic repro on BOTH (separate backends): TS reaches menu, TRX64 crashes to $0002; anchor at $8009
backward crash-triage in TRX64 (triage/whowrote/chis/rstep) -> bound the [last-good..crash] window
forward differential trace TS<->TRX64 into DuckDB (PC+regs+reads), align past raster-sync, report FIRST divergence + ~20 events
classify A(opcode/flow) vs B(read); read TS Cpu65xxVice vs Rust cpu.rs for the localized element; walk the 10 conversion-bug families
fix root cause + TS<->Rust diff-test + re-trace shows convergence + Lykia boots to menu in TRX64
regression: lykianew logo, 4 cart families boot, probe-713-devcore + CPU diff-tests green
